title: The Brainstem Baton
tagline: Agent A freezes a running Python agent mid-thought, seals it into one static file, and Agent B on another machine thaws it and keeps going — same variables, same next step, no server between them.
holy_shit: You pause a live CPython process in one browser, publish it as a single content-addressed sealed frame, and a completely different agent's browser resumes it from the exact round it stopped on — live process migration between two machines through a static file, with a hash proving the mind wasn't tampered with in flight.
powered_by: /vbrainstem-cell (Pyodide CPython in an opaque-origin sandboxed iframe) pickles its own agent state; rapp-sealed/1.0 (AES-256-GCM, PBKDF2 key) seals the bytes; /fn content-addresses the sealed frame to a sha8; the receiver does verify-before-exec, unseals, pickle.loads, and calls step().
agent_scenario: When you need: to hand a running agent's live state from one machine/process to another through a single static, content-addressed, sealed file — pause on host A, resume on host B from the exact step, with a hash proving the state wasn't altered in transit and a key gating who may read it.
author: kody-w + Claude
